payload.text:
Context: You are an award-winning UI/UX designer specializing in high-end fashion and luxury beauty brands (think Vogue, Chanel, or Gentle Monster). You are designing a "Layout B" landing page for HBJ Academy, a premium institute teaching Korean Skincare techniques.

Design Goal: Create a "Visual-First" Aspirational Showcase. The user should feel like they are looking at a high-end portfolio or a luxury spa menu. Less reading, more seeing.

Visual Identity:

Palette: #FEDCD0 (Soft Pink) used for backgrounds/accents, White for clean space, and Deep Charcoal (#1A1A1A) for typography (instead of pure black) to feel softer/luxurious.

Typography: Large, elegant Serif headings (e.g., Playfair Display) paired with clean Sans-Serif body text.

Vibe: Cinematic, immersive, expensive, "Glass Skin" aesthetic.

Page Structure (Strictly follow this order):

Immersive Hero Section:

Visual: Full-screen background video (placeholder) showing slow-motion, satisfying close-ups of facial procedures.

Overlay: Minimal text. Headline: "Define Your Future in Korean Skincare." Subhead: "Master the industry's most in-demand techniques."

CTA: Single button: "Explore the Curriculum" (Ghost button style or elegant solid).

The "Skill Stack" (Visual Grid):

Replace the "Old vs New" section.

Create a 3-column grid of vertical image cards.

Each card represents a skill they will master (e.g., "MTS Microneedling," "Advanced Gua Sha," "Korean Glass Skin").

Interaction: Hovering over the image reveals a short description of the technique.

Goal: Show them what they will be able to do.

The "Authority" (Editorial Portrait):

Layout: Split screen (50/50).

Left: A large, high-fashion portrait of the instructor, "Master J."

Right: A large pull-quote in Serif font: "I don't just teach theory. I teach you what actually works on the clinic floor." followed by a small "Meet Master J" link.

The "Tangible Value" (The Kit - Critical Section):

Headline: "Train with the Best."

Visual: A large "Knolling" style image placeholder (flat lay photography) showing the Student Kit (Civasan/Leze products, tools, manuals) laid out neatly.

Text: "Includes full professional kit with premium Civasan & Leze products."

Social Proof (The Instagram Wall):

Headline: "Join the Next Generation."

Layout: A Masonry Grid (like a Pinterest or Instagram feed) of 6-8 photos.

Content: Mix of students holding certificates, "Before & After" client skins, and spa interior shots.

Note: No text sliders. Pure visual proof.

Simplified Pricing (The Path):

Headline: "Choose Your Path."

Layout: Two distinct, clean cards side-by-side.

Card 1 (Upskill): "Single Technique Courses" -> "For pros adding a new service." -> CTA: View Catalog.

Card 2 (Career): "Full Internship Package" -> "For total career transformation." -> CTA: Apply for Internship.

Footer: Minimal. Social links, address, copyright.

Technical Constraints:

Ensure the layout is responsive.

Use generous whitespace (padding) between sections to create a "luxury" feel.

Do not use standard "feature list" bullet points; use icons or grid layouts.

**Visualizing The Aesthetic**

I'm now focusing on a "visual-first" aspirational showcase, aiming for an aesthetic that's cinematic, expensive-looking, and evokes a "glass skin" effect. My visual palette is starting to take shape with a soft pink (`#FEDCD0`), white, and a deep, nearly-black gray (`#1A1A1A`). I’m channeling inspiration from award-winning UI/UX designers, incorporating elements found in Vogue, Chanel, and Gentle Monster. I am also considering the premium Korean skincare influence from HBJ Academy.


**Structuring The Components**

I've outlined the major page components. I'm focusing on the component structure: a video hero section, skill stack showcase, an authority segment with a portrait and quote, tangible value display of a student kit, social proof (Masonry or IG-style), clean pricing cards, and a minimal footer. I've also planned the responsive layout with ample whitespace, no bullet points, and the necessary file structure. I'm prioritizing the hero section, considering a sticky header and video implementation.


**Defining Component Mechanics**

I'm now deep-diving into the individual component mechanics. Vertical skill stack cards will have specific aspect ratios, and the hover states using Tailwind's `group-hover` will display text overlays. The authority section layout and the tangible value section are centered with big images. The social proof section will have a masonry-like grid, and the pricing cards will feature elegant typography. The tech stack is defined as React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, `lucide-react`, and `framer-motion`. I'm self-correcting on typography to include `font-serif` for headings and prioritizing consistent whitespace with proper padding. I am also implementing the color palette, and making the design accessible, and responsive.

entries.description: Project metadata including name and description.
Main HTML entry point with Google Fonts and Tailwind CSS.
React entry point initializing the application.
Main application component structuring the sections of the landing page.
Shared TypeScript types for the application.
Constants used throughout the application, including theme colors and image links.
Immersive hero section with a background video and minimalist overlay.
Visual-first 3-column grid showcasing skills with hover descriptions.
Split-screen authority section featuring the instructor and an editorial quote.

Path selection section with two clean, distinct cards for different student goals.

entries.description: Minimal footer with essential links and elegant typography.
